reforms.description: Innovation policy should provide more support for organisational change, enhance collaboration between business and researchers, and foster more rapid diffusion of knowledge. This refocusing aims to encourage investments in organisational change, particularly in service sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing collaboration and supporting organisational change, the reform is expected to increase productivity and innovation in service sectors, which are crucial for economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is aimed at increasing productivity growth through enhanced innovation policy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in innovation policy focus, aiming to improve collaboration and organisational change, which are essential for enhancing productivity in the economy.
reforms.source_quote: Innovation policy should provide more support for organisational change, enhance collaboration between business and researchers and foster a broader and more rapid diffusion of knowledge.

reforms.description: Belgium aims to strengthen its national innovation system to enhance productivity growth, particularly by removing barriers to competition in ICT-using sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the national innovation system is expected to foster productivity growth, which can lead to higher economic output and efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The need to strengthen the national innovation system is discussed as a key policy challenge.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses systemic issues in the innovation landscape, which are crucial for long-term economic growth and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: It would also be important to strengthen the national innovation system and improve education achievement.

reforms.description: The Belgian government aims to modernize public administration and implement e-government strategies, which will lead to a more innovative and efficient administration, improving service delivery and creating opportunities for cost savings and productivity gains.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the efficiency of public spending can lead to lower taxes on labor income, thereby improving incentives to work and contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has already taken reforms to raise the efficiency of public administration.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to improve public sector efficiency and service delivery, which is crucial for long-term fiscal sustainability.
reforms.source_quote: Further reforms are planned to modernise the public administration and to implement the e-government strategies of the federal government, the regions and the communities.

reforms.description: Belgium has made significant progress in implementing e-government services, which aim to improve efficiency and reduce administrative burdens through better data sharing and online transactions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: E-government initiatives streamline processes, reduce costs, and enhance service delivery, contributing to overall economic efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: Belgium has made considerable progress in its implementation of e-government services since 2001.
reforms.importance_rationale: The e-government initiative represents a structural change in how public services are delivered, enhancing efficiency and accessibility for citizens and businesses.
reforms.source_quote: Belgium has made considerable progress in its implementation of e-government services since 2001 and is now firmly positioned within a large cluster of countries with an e-government maturity score between 50 and 60%.

reforms.description: The Federal Centre of Expertise was created within the Ministry of Public Health to provide technical and scientific analysis to support decision-making in health technology assessments, aiming to improve efficiency in healthcare delivery.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating systematic health technology assessments, the centre aims to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of healthcare delivery, potentially leading to better health outcomes and cost savings.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the Federal Centre of Expertise represents a significant step towards improving the use of evidence in healthcare decision-making, which is crucial for enhancing overall system efficiency.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has created the Federal Centre of Expertise within the Ministry of Public Health in order to provide technical and scientific analysis in support of decision-making.

reforms.description: The government will ensure non-discriminatory access to the new wage tax reduction for private companies cooperating with public research institutions, including those abroad, to enhance public-private partnerships in research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates collaboration between private firms and research institutions, potentially leading to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to ensuring access to the wage tax reduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform enhances existing tax incentives for collaboration between private and public sectors, which is significant for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government should ensure non-discriminatory access to the new wage tax reduction for private companies co-operating with public research institutions.

reforms.description: Innovation policies will be refocused to encourage more investments in organizational change, aiming to enhance productivity and adaptability in the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging investments in organizational change can lead to improved efficiency and productivity across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to refocusing innovation policies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in innovation policy focus, which can have meaningful impacts on productivity.
reforms.source_quote: Innovation policies should be refocused to encourage more investments in organisational change.

reforms.description: The government has initiated a programme of administrative reform aimed at reducing the administrative burden on businesses, particularly focusing on labour-market regulations, to stimulate economic activity and reduce costs for the private sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By lowering administrative costs, the reform is expected to enhance business entry and competition, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The administrative burden is gradually being lowered as indicated by national bi-annual surveys.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant effort to streamline regulations affecting businesses, which can have a meaningful impact on the overall business environment and innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The government has embarked on a programme of administrative reform aimed at achieving the objectives of regulation at a lower cost to the private sector.

reforms.description: The Belgian government set an ambitious target to reduce the time required to register a limited liability company from 56 working days to just 3 days by July 2005, implementing a broad-based strategy that includes the creation of one-stop shops for administrative formalities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By significantly reducing the time and complexity involved in starting a business, this reform encourages entrepreneurship and innovation, which can lead to increased economic activity and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: The registration time has been reduced to 27 days as of October 2004, with further reductions planned.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the regulatory environment for startups, aiming to foster a more conducive atmosphere for entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The government set itself the ambitious target of bringing down this number to 3 days by 1 July 2005 and has developed a broad-based strategy to reach this goal.

reforms.description: The Belgian government has introduced new tax incentives for R&D, extending benefits to private companies collaborating with public research institutions, thereby encouraging greater investment in R&D across sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing R&D investment, the reform is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The measure was extended to private companies as of October 2005.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the approach to R&D funding, broadening the scope of tax incentives to stimulate private sector investment in research.
reforms.source_quote: The government has decided to further extend it to private companies that collaborate with a public research institution as of October 2005.

reforms.description: The Flemish Region has implemented a program to promote greater job mobility in R&D, supporting firms that encourage mobility and partnerships with universities to enhance research capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing job mobility is expected to reduce labor market mismatches and improve the attractiveness of R&D careers, thereby increasing the supply of skilled researchers.
reforms.status_evidence: The authorities have identified greater job mobility as one answer to the risk of labour market shortage.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative addresses a critical issue in the R&D labor market, aiming to enhance the supply of skilled workers through improved mobility and partnerships.
reforms.source_quote: The authorities have identified greater job mobility as one answer to the risk of labour market shortage.

reforms.description: The Flemish government has increased public expenditure on tertiary education, aiming to enhance the funding available for universities to cope with growing demands and international competition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for universities is expected to improve educational quality and research output, thereby enhancing human capital and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Flemish government will increase public expenditure on tertiary education over the coming years.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in funding aimed at improving the quality of higher education, which is crucial for maintaining competitiveness in research and education.
reforms.source_quote: While the Flemish government will increase public expenditure on tertiary education over the coming years...

reforms.description: The Flemish Community has made research grants more competitive by introducing new criteria that emphasize publications and citations, thereby incentivizing higher research quality.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By linking funding to research quality, this reform is expected to enhance the overall output and impact of academic research.
reforms.status_evidence: The former criteria were recently made more competitive by adding a publications and citations criterion.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly alters the funding landscape for research, promoting a focus on quality and impact, which is essential for advancing knowledge and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The former were recently made more competitive by adding a publications and citations criterion...

reforms.description: The Council of Ministers adopted instruments for the creation of start-up funds, enabling loans for individuals setting up businesses or those in business for less than four years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates access to finance for new businesses, which can enhance innovation and job creation.
reforms.status_evidence: Instruments for the creation of start-up funds were adopted by the Council of Ministers.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of start-up funds represents a significant step towards supporting entrepreneurship and innovation in Belgium.
reforms.source_quote: The Council of Ministers adopts instruments for the creation of start-up funds (Fonds Starters) so that loans can be made available through the Fonds de participation to those setting up in business or who have been in business less than 4 years.

reforms.description: A new exemption from payment of half the wage tax for research assistants or post-doctorate staff employed by universities and higher education establishments was introduced.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages the hiring of research staff, thereby enhancing the capacity for applied research and innovation in higher education.
reforms.status_evidence: The exemption from payment of half the wage tax for research assistants was enacted.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly supports the research ecosystem by reducing costs associated with hiring research personnel.
reforms.source_quote: As of 1 October entry into force of exemption from payment of half the wage tax for research assistants or post-doctorate staff employed by Universities and various higher education establishments.
